mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% indicates a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) above 105 indicates rising supply and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) and the number of Housing Starts (HOUST) indicates rising demand and housing market activity, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 1% and HOUST rises > 5% in 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 4.5% and a simultaneous increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) above 2% indicates an easing monetary cycle with rising inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rises above 2%
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% and a simultaneous increase in the number of Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) above 200,000 indicates a tightening labor market with rising employment, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.5% and PAYEMS rises above 200,000
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the Real Disposable Personal Income (RSAFS) below -1% indicates falling demand and consumer spending, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in low-demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
